nativeRange.habitat: Deeper fore-reef slopes, generally 27-180 m, often near black coral thickets and steep drop-offs

diet.notes: Generally accepts a varied carnivorous diet in captivity, including enriched mysis shrimp, enriched brine shrimp, and finely chopped meaty seafoods, with some vegetable matter (nori or marine algae preparations) for balance. Multiple small feedings per day are commonly recommended for newly imported specimens, which can be initially shy at the feed.

avoidWith: soft corals and mushrooms (may be nipped)
very aggressive tankmates (triggers, large angels) that out-compete at feeding

summary: A peaceful, mesophotic Central Pacific butterflyfish recognised for its yellow-and-white body with bold black caudal cap. Generally considered one of the more adaptable butterflyfishes in captivity but is not reef safe, and prefers moderately lit deeper-style aquascapes.
careNotes:
Tinkeri Butterflyfish naturally occur on deeper fore-reef slopes (commonly 27-180 m) and tend to do best in aquariums that mimic those conditions: subdued to moderate lighting, mature live rock with caves and overhangs, and a relatively quiet community. They have been reported to do well in well-established systems of at least roughly 120 US gal (around 450-500 L), with longer footprints favoured over taller tanks to provide horizontal swimming space.

The species is generally considered NOT reef-safe — sources describe it as likely to pick at mushrooms, anemones, and stony coral polyps, though individual variability is reported. It is best housed in fish-only or fish-with-live-rock setups, or in lightly stocked reefs where the keeper accepts the risk.

Like most Chaetodon butterflyfishes, Tinkeri tend to form long-term monogamous pairs in the wild; bonded pairs are commonly offered in the trade and generally do well when introduced together. Singles also acclimate readily. New arrivals can be initially shy and should be offered small, frequent feedings of enriched frozen foods to encourage feeding.

This species commands high prices in the aquarium trade due to its depth-restricted collection; sources note collectors must dive well below typical recreational reef depths.
breedingNotes: Wild Tinkeri butterflyfish are oviparous broadcast spawners that pair-bond and release pelagic eggs; there is no reported documentation of routine captive breeding for this species.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Husbandry parameters (tank size, temp, pH, SG, dKH, temperament, reef compatibility, diet) extracted from the species product page.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked maximum size (15 cm TL vs LiveAquaria's 5.5 in / ~14 cm — close agreement, averaged to 13-15 cm), confirmed Chaetodontidae / Acanthuriformes taxonomy, distribution (Hawaii, Johnston, Marshall Islands), and deep-reef habitat (27-180 m). FishBase trophic level 3.4 confirmed planktonic + benthic invertebrate diet (treated as omnivore).
